channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Experts warn that Trump’s use of AI images pushes new boundaries
item.description: The Trump administration has not shied away from sharing AI-generated imagery online, embracing cartoonlike visuals and memes and promoting them on official White House channels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: This new privacy-focused phone service is designed to keep your phone from getting hacked
item.description: A privacy-centric cellphone carrier called Cape is now officially available across the United States, offering a unique set of features to protect users from surveillance and identity theft.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Millions of smartphone users could be at risk from the SMS sign-in URL issue - here's what you need to look out for
item.description: SMS sign-in links frequently expose sensitive data through weak authentication and long-lived, easily guessed URLs.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Power shortages, carbon capture, and AI automation: What’s ahead for data centers in 2026
item.description: The data center industry is racing toward a power crisis that technology alone will not be able to solve, according to Uptime Institute’s 2026 data center predictions report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Is AI adoption at work flatlining? This major survey thinks so - but it's definitely not the end just yet
item.description: The number of people using AI might be flatlining, but it looks like we'll see more uses cases develop next.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: French authorities to ban Teams, Zoom, other video apps for gov’t use
item.description: France plans to phase out American video conferencing services such as Zoom and Microsoft Teams within government agencies and replace them with its own proprietary tool, Visio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tesla’s price targets rise even as its profit outlook tanks
item.description: Tesla’s stock, which raced to an all-time high in December before retreating some, trades at more than 195 times its expected earnings over the next 12 months as of Monday’s close.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: For some reason, Bethesda has teamed up with Pringles to launch a Fallout 76 Mystery Flavour
item.description: Pringles has launched a new Fallout 76 Mystery Flavour, and to celebrate, fans can submit their guess to win a special prize.
item.dc:creator: Demi Williams, TechRadar

item.title: Independent auditors confirm Surfshark’s VPN infrastructure as secure
item.description: Surfshark has completed a comprehensive independent security audit of its network infrastructure. SecuRing found no critical vulnerabilities, reinforcing the provider's claims of resilience against sophisticated cyberattacks.
item.dc:creator: Rene Millman, TechRadar

item.title: AMD could be working on a way to minimize RAM price hike pain
item.description: An official collaboration between AMD, V-Color and Cooler Master hints at more possible bundle deals in the future.
item.dc:creator: Darren Allan, TechRadar

item.title: US Army signs multi-billion dollar Salesforce deal - giving military recruiters access to Slack
item.description: Salesforce will provide productivity and collaboration software to the US Army for five to ten years in a multibillion-dollar deal.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Alibaba’s Qwen3-Max-Thinking expands enterprise AI model choices
item.description: Alibaba Cloud’s latest AI model, Qwen3-Max-Thinking, is staking a claim as one of the world’s most advanced reasoning engines after posting benchmark results that delivered competitive results against leading models from Google and OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Descope introduces Agentic Identity Hub 2.0 for managing AI agents
item.description: Descope has announced Agentic Identity Hub 2.0, an update to its no-code identity platform for AI agents and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why the European Union is launching a probe into Elon Musk’s Grok chatbot
item.description: The European Union opened a formal investigation into Elon Musk’s social media platform X on Monday after his artificial intelligence chatbot Grok spewed nonconsensual sexualized deepfake images on the platform.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Tim Cook slammed for partying at Melania Trump’s movie after Pretti killing
item.description: While at a White House screening of the first lady's documentary, the Apple CEO posed with the director, who's been accused of sexual misconduct.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: How Flights for Freedom is flying trafficked children to safety
item.description: In 2023, Ken Lux found himself in an FBI briefing on child trafficking. The CEO of Luxe Aviation was there as the past commander of the Sacramento County Sheriff’s Office’s Air Squadron, a volunteer cadre of 50 general aviation pilots supporting police missions and community service.
item.dc:creator: Susan Karlin, Fast Company

item.title: Photonic chip vendor snags Gates investment
item.description: Neurophos has landed another $110 million in funding from investors including Gates Frontier, Bill Gates’ venture capital outfit, to move its photonic chips nearer to production. The growing use of AI has led to increasing demands on computational power within data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia is still working with suppliers on RAM chips for Rubin
item.description: Nvidia changed its requirements for suppliers of the next generation of high-bandwidth memory, HBM4, but is close to certifying revised chips from Samsung Electronics for use in its AI systems, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Intel: Latest news and insights
item.description: More processor coverage on Network World: AMD news and insights | Nvidia news and insights Intel is hoping for a turnaround under its new CEO, Lip-BuTan. Intel’s Q1 2025 revenue was $12.7 billion, flat year-over-year.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: With AI, the database matters again
item.description: Developers have spent the past decade trying to forget databases exist. Not literally, of course. We still store petabytes. But for the average developer, the database became an implementation detail; an essential but staid utility layer we worked hard not to think about.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Winter storms can knock out your tech fast: Prepare now
item.description: Major winter storm threatens widespread power outages across the US starting Friday. Essential tech prep tips to keep devices charged and stay connected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Agentic AI exposes what we’re doing wrong
item.description: We’ve spent the last decade telling ourselves that cloud computing is mostly a tool problem. Pick a provider, standardize a landing zone, automate deployments, and you’re “modern.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Python picks up speed with a new JIT
item.description: Faster Python is now within reach, thanks to its shiny new JIT. But we also have fast dataframes thanks to Pandas, and three slick new GUI interfaces for SQLite. And then there’s Zed, the new Rust-powered contender to VS Code’s throne.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intel nabs Qualcomm veteran to lead GPU initiative
item.description: Intel and Qualcomm have traded executives several times over the years, and the trading continues with Intel luring away Qualcomm’s GPU lead. Eric Demers, a long-time industry veteran GPU engineer, joins Intel as senior vice president and the lead of its GPU engineering team.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI can’t fix a broken NetOps practice
item.description: Only 35% of enterprise IT organizations have been completely successful with applying AI to network management, according to new research on AI-driven NetOps strategies from Enterprise Management Associates (EMA).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ahead of filing season, IRS scraps customer service metric it’s used for 20 years
item.description: An IRS watchdog told Congress last year that the discarded metric is “misleading,” and "does not accurately reflect the experience of most taxpayers who call." The post Ahead of filing season, IRS scraps customer service metric it’s used for 20 years first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network
